title: <<COMPONENTNAME>> component configuration
description: The configuration <<COMPONENTNAME>> itself understands: the object at `component.global`, with each entry of `component.instances[]` validated against `#/$defs/route`. A processor models ONE ROUTE PER INSTANCE — a set of topic filters, a pipeline of stages, and a target. The library envelope (logging, messaging, metricEmission, heartbeat, streaming, credentials, parameters, health, hierarchy, identity, topic, tags) is owned by the canonical schema and is deliberately not redeclared here. Keep `additionalProperties: false` so a typo'd key is caught at deploy time rather than silently ignored.

defaults.description: Defaults applied to every route that does not override them.

tickMs.description: How often stateful stages are ticked. A stage that emits on time rather than on arrival (a window, a batch, a debounce) produces its output on this cadence.

maxQueue.description: How many messages may be queued for a route before new ones are dropped and counted. Bounded on purpose: an unbounded queue does not remove backpressure, it relocates the failure to the heap.

route.title: One route
route.description: One entry of `component.instances[]`. Routes are independent — one loop each — so a slow route cannot stall another, and per-key state inside a stage needs no coordination.

id.description: Unique route id. It is the `{instance}` token of this route's UNS topics, so it must be a valid UNS token (lower-kebab).

subscribe.description: Topic filters this route consumes. Wildcards are allowed.

publishTopic.description: The topic the transformed result is published on. A processor is payload-agnostic and republishes onto a topic its config names — which is why it uses raw messaging() rather than the data() facade, whose topic and body shape are its own. Config templates are resolved here, so `{ThingName}`, `{ComponentName}`, a hierarchy level, or a tag key may be interpolated (e.g. `ecv1/{ThingName}/my-processor/rollup/app/summary`) and a deployed route addresses the device it actually landed on.

target.description: Where the result goes. `local` = the device-local bus. `northbound` = the northbound broker.

pipeline.description: The stages, in order. Each takes a message and returns zero or more messages, so a stage can filter, map, or fan out. An empty pipeline is a pass-through republisher.

maxQueue.description: Per-route override of the queue bound.

tickMs.description: Per-route override of the stage tick cadence.

stage.title: One pipeline stage
stage.description: A single-key object naming the stage and its arguments. Add your own here as you implement it in `src/proc.ts`.

fieldEquals.description: Keep only messages whose dotted body path equals a value; drop the rest.

path.description: A dotted path into the message body, e.g. `signal.id`.

value.description: The value the path must equal. Any JSON type.

countPerTick.description: Accumulate arrivals and emit one rollup per tick. The stateful half of the stage interface: it emits nothing on arrival and produces its output in `onTick`. Windows, batches and debounces are all this shape.
